bug_report.summary: Debugger > Monitors: topmost Tree row label disappears when scrolled near the header area
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Godot editor and run a project so debugger data is live.
Open Debugger bottom panel → Monitors tab.
Scroll/drag the Monitors list so the first visible row is very close to the top/header (just under the tab strip/column header).
expected_behaviors: The first row near the top (e.g., the group header row such as “Object”) should remain visible, only being clipped when it would actually underlap the header area.
actual_behaviors: The topmost row label (e.g., “Object”) disappears entirely at the top edge while its child rows remain visible, suggesting over-aggressive culling/clipping near the header.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Scene dock Tree view: left-side row indicator/guide for selected node renders differently after change
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the Godot editor and open/create a project.
Create a new scene with a Control root and add a Tree node as its child.
In the Scene dock, click the Tree node row to select it.
Press F2 (rename) and/or triple-click the node name field (as in the scenario).
Observe the left edge of the selected row (indent/guide/indicator area just left of the node icon/text).
expected_behaviors: The selected row’s left-side indicator/indent/guide rendering should match the pre-change build (no extra/missing pixels or altered highlight).
actual_behaviors: The left-side indicator/indent/guide area for the selected Tree node row shows a small but visible rendering difference compared to the pre-change build (pixel-level change within bbox [43,161]-[52,182]).
